features.summary: AMD delivered record Q3 2021 results with revenue of $4.3 billion, up 54% year-over-year, marking the fifth consecutive quarter of 50%+ growth. Computing and Graphics segment grew 44% to $2.4 billion driven by Ryzen processors and graphics cards, while Enterprise, Embedded and Semi-Custom segment surged 69% to $1.9 billion led by EPYC server processors and gaming console demand. Server revenue more than doubled year-over-year for the sixth straight quarter, with data center business now representing mid-20s percent of total revenue. Gross margin expanded 440 basis points to 48% and operating income doubled to $1.06 billion.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year 2021 revenue growth guidance to approximately 65% from 60% previously, with Q4 expected at $4.5 billion (39% year-over-year growth). They anticipate continued strength in server and semi-custom gaming console sales in Q4, while PC market is expected to remain flattish due to supply chain constraints around matched sets. For 2022, management sees positive demand environment with multiple growth drivers including data center expansion, graphics momentum, and strong console demand continuing into the third year of the cycle, though they model PC market as potentially flat.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints remain a key challenge, particularly around matched sets in the PC market which may continue into the first half of 2022. The company faces intensifying competition in the server market as competitors plan new product launches in early 2022. Rising foundry costs and general cost inflation present margin pressure risks. Regulatory approval for the Xilinx acquisition, while progressing well, still needs final completion by year-end as planned.
features.key_drivers: Data center business momentum continues with server revenue doubling year-over-year and strong adoption of third-generation EPYC processors across cloud hyperscalers and enterprise customers. Gaming remains robust with semi-custom console revenue growing and graphics card demand strong in the channel. The pending Xilinx acquisition will expand AMD's addressable market into adaptive computing, AI, communications, industrial and automotive segments. Strong product portfolio positioning with competitive advantages in performance and efficiency across CPU and GPU offerings drives market share gains.
